name: Hiawatha First Nation - Heath and Social Services
description: Beautiful place

name: Markham Eglinton Pharmacy
Good Shepherd Ministries - Toronto - Good Shepherd Centre
address: 412 Queen St E, Toronto, ON M5A 1T3
description:
Complete disorganized service and horrible workers

What kinda social worker says “ you have to call back I have some agitated client at the window and hangs up” no explanation no reason. I been calling them about taxes since Tuesday. These people will just hang up and ignore your request.

This is an amazing spot on Queen West that serves hot meals to the homeless everyday from 2-4pm. The staff is an amazing bunch of people that truly care about the clients as it shows in the everyday care and effort they put in.

This individual should not be allowed to work as a social worker, the rudeness when you ask him basic questions it’s beyond comprehension . I felt mentally harassed for the way he treated me.

I went for breakfast but I was 90 minutes early. I was severely dehydrated and starving for protein. They gave me 2 protein drinks, and chocolate to hold me over. 90 minutes later and feeling much better they gave me a high protein breakfast and allowed me to select a 3rd drink. More protein then I get in several meals at the other shelters. 2 thumbs up and thank you so very much for being a humanitarian in a city laden with barriers and rules that don't apply to all. Thank you again... nice to see one shelter that treats the humans as humans. Im not a religious man, God bless you.

Nice volunteers and staff, good food and cafeteria setting. Washrooms could be better stocked and cleaned.
Great place to get a good meal, a drink or pick up food.

name: Society of Saint Vincent de Paul - Toronto - Church St - Toronto Central Council - Vincenpaul Community Homes
description:
If they focused on housing as much as they focused on recycling people back onto the street, I think there'd be more people actually moving forward. Some clients are favoured, and spend north of a year in their house, while others who follow the rules and truly do their best are cut off before they're really ready, psychologically or financially. They give up on you unexpectedly. Pretty disgusting, considering they get paid either way.

If you are looking to donate clothing, this is a great place to do so. It is tucked away next to the church so you will have to knock on the door or ring the doorbell for access. The community workers were very kind.

I know they are busy at st Boniface, I have a disability and it has been 3 weeks, the head office informed me that there is nothing that can be done. At least other people are getting help and I appreciate the effort. Sorry if I have been a nuisance and will not bother you again

My account was hacked and I apologize for any rude comments that came from that .  Saint Vincent De Paul is a wonderful and Blessed organization .That does much good for many people and communities

One year, Saint Vincent was fabulous....gave a food coupon, very appreciated.  This year, I was told to phone the church for one....in several calls, no one has answered the phone and there is  no voicemail.

name: Algoma Family Services - Elliot Lake
Algoma Family Services - Wawa
description:
Afs has been amazing for my daughter.

This place don’t even deserve a star especially there access center the head supervisor don’t know what he is doing he only cares about the benefits and money and was clear about it and when then other family started the conflict he threatened to terminate my contract because they know I have no place to go till court and he gave me the wrong schedule and apparently that was my fault and almost didn’t see my son because of his mistake you don’t belong in this industry if u can’t care about the family life’s that is in ur hands and don’t care if u destroy a family even more then they already are this job isn’t just about money it’s about saving families not destroying them

name: North Bay Regional Health Centre - Sudbury Campus
description:
Don't let the perfectly kempt gardens and grounds fool you; the inside of the building is absolutely not maintained at a proper health standard for the patients. For example, during my 8 days at Kirkwood the main shower for patients was not cleaned even a single time. The showers are covered in pink and black mold. On my last day there I cleaned the pink mold off myself as I could not leave allowing people to think this is what they deserve. Patients deserve a clean and dignified area to heal in. Within my first 5 minutes there, a male nurse walked into my room unannounced while I was changing into hospital clothes. When I reported it to my female nurse, I was laughed at.

I recently was a patient of the Kirkwood Inpatient Adult Psyshiatric Hospital/Floor 5. I had an incredibly positive experience and they helped me through a severe depressive episode. Through my almost two weeks there, I had only positive experiences with the docotrs/psychiatrists, nurses, cleaning and kitchen staff. Everyone was very kind, positive and wanted to help. Although it didn't always feel like it was helping, it definitely put me back on my feet. The only negative being the short visitation times (30 minutes) and limited access to personal technology - but I understand! Thank you Kirkwood.

The doctors are very friendly but the secretary is so incredibly rude, I've gotten nothing but sarcasm from her and eye rolls, I've also seen her interact with two people in front of me the same way. If you come here watch out for the old, brown haired secretary with glasses. The last thing a office needs is a rude secretary which is the face of the office and the first person you greet.

Never stayed at Kirkwood place, but was in Hospital for a few weeks. I had a private suite, and the staff were wonderful. I was allowed to use my iPod anytime I wanted. No phones, but there were computers to use if needed. I received the BEST help ever.
Dr. Okoronkwo is a fantastic phyciatrist. His office for outpatients is horrible. The wait times can be up to 4hrs even with a scheduled appointment. Bring a book folks.
I am no longer a patient, but have loads of respect. Take good care of yourself!

If I could put zero stars I would, the service is terrible, they don't even consider a patient's time. I've sat and waited over an hour and a half for my scheduled appointments every time I come in and they don't even call to let you know they're bookings are backed up.

name: Crisis Intervention Centre (Belleville General Hospital)
description:
I was in crisis on the 24th of May 2022 and this was such a miserable phone call for me to partake in.
I had been trying to contact my doctor for hours for help to no avail, no walk in clinic would take me, I had already cried in my pharmacy asking for any advice they could give and I was on what felt like my last leg. I couldn't sit in the ER for hours in the condition I was in, so I called the crisis intervention centre.
The lady who answered immediately broke the news to me that there is currently no appointments being held and that I can just do a couple of CBT-esque exercises to calm myself out of a panic attack and that if I am unable to keep myself safe, to go to the ER. She had no other options to offer me. But this wasn't a panic attack.

Upon hanging up, I go back to the BGH website. Nowhere does it say they are not open to appointments at all, in fact it encourages you to give them a call. Which I probably will never do again.

I cry. I cry for what feels like the entire day, and basically was the entire day. I poured my soul out to so many people that day, begging for help. I couldn't go to the ER, I was too exhausted to hurt myself at that point anyways. I dragged my body home, got into bed and didn't leave it until the next day. I woke up and continued to cry.

I needed help and even the crisis intervention centre wouldn't help me.

I had cried so hard and for so long, my face is still raw 2 days later.

I know this isn't everyone's experience, but this was mine. And people should know.

Edit due to owners response, May 26th 2022:

The woman I talked to on the phone was the very first person I spoke to. The only person I spoke to. I asked if there was anyone available today i could speak to and all she told me was they aren't doing appointments. She did not give me any information I could go off of to infer that virtual appointments were an option. And if she had, I would have asked for one. She said there was no appointments, a general statement which made me believe I had to go elsewhere for help. Whether she could have worded it better or not, the only option she offered me was a tip to relax a panic attack and the ER. If there were more options, I wish she had told me.

Wonderful Staff. Thank you for being there for me when I need it the most.

name: Lanark, Leeds & Greenville Distress Centre
description:
service is very nice and helpful thank you Augustin

Augustine good service

Augustine, Amisha, Pravesh, ck chef, Ram, Sanjit

name: Gerstein Centre
address: 100 Charles Street East, Toronto, ON M4Y 1V3
description:
The lady who answered my call was very dismissive and rude. She  said “what do you want me to do about that I have other callers on the queue” in a very dismissive and rude tone when I called to vent about a situation. The workers should be more empathetic. Nothing about the call helped my situation.

FRESH program (more so rotten due to my experience) was absolutely not as good as I'd hoped (putting it lightly). putting it harshly (and truthfully) it was awful, futile, boring, frustrating in execution and the facilitator Steve and the cast is seriously such a bunch of duds who I didn't enjoy at all whatsoever. what a bust. i do not love who joins as clients because most were a very older demographic which i could hardly ever relate to and even worse if foreign and you can barely understand them. majority isn't younger in age like myself so if i am not feeling apart of something because of that, you have to expect i won't take it well. Not fun at all and for over a month I wasted my time ever being involved. I'm glad I said what I said to Steve even the way I handled it I wouldn't take back and don't get me started on his boss whom I hate with a passion and have no respect for taking his employee's word for something he never witnessed which was a false incident report. Well guess what Michael Aucoin, your employee deserved how I spoke to him and wasn't nearly as bad as I could've had it be. I'm glad you were so frustrated you hung up mid call because your "warning" doesn't mean anything to me and I declared I was out before that. I don't care what you think and you don't run me like you run others. So you aren't going to tell me what I'm going to do and expect me to just go with it nonchalantly. Oh yeah also the group hike was supposed to happen already and was canceled twice due to weather so they can't even keep their word because they're so messy at scheduling so I knew then I was over it because I just refuse to take them seriously if they can't get themselves in order. I have zero patience for this. Way to lose a client and cause a bridge to be burnt

DANGER! DO NOT GO HERE

The crisis workers answering phones are incredibly unprofessional. I was accused of yelling and explaining how to "do their job" before being hung up on. I went through 3 seperate workers who all took my crisis very personally. This is not the first time I've experienced issues. I was kicked out of their facility for giving attitude to a staff... Mind you I had been kicked out of my place in the middle of the pandemic. I was just a kid in crisis. I was recommended by police to.go to them.

I regret this decision to.this.day as it has really soured my opinion of the MH system.

If they can't understand you cause you're crying, they hang up. If you're speaking too fast, they will tell you to calm down until they hang up.

Crisis aren't meant to be pretty, Crisis workers are trained to de escalate and provide safety during the lowest points in people's lives where they are struggling to help themselves. I have yet to experience a staff member at this facility who can implement these skills effectively in a crisis.

EDIT: multiple 1 star reviews of clients saying they were hung up on from crisis team all.the way to head management. This is very concerning and UNSAFE.

Please report your experiences to the City of Toronto. I believe this is a city run, charity type centre. Please advocate for safety.

THIS PLACE IS NOT SAFE FOR THOSE IN CRISIS. PLEASE GO TO THE HOSPITAL.

it's not perfect at hospital, but at least you'll be safe and have access to more care options! Take care of you!! Not the crisis workers! They should be taking care of themselves so they are readily available to help.

I called this crisis center because I was struggling with severe depression, insomnia and work-related stress. The crisis worker who responded to my call was very dismissive and rude, and I ended up feeling worse. I cried while on the phone and I didn't think that I was going to make it through the night. It's honestly made me really scared to reach out for help elsewhere because of the bad experience I had. I didn't get the worker's name, but I hope that in the future she can show more empathy because it might seriously hurt someone out there.

Great people to talk to but honestly they judge more then you judge your self so no o will not be calling again thanks

Amazing service, the staff are wonderful, caring, and very knowledgeable. They make you feel like you are part of the family. I would highly recommend going if you are in need, they will do anything they possibly can to help you. They deserve more than 5 stars for going above and beyond.

Best staff always they go above and beyound there staff is always pollite

name: KFL&A Public Health
description:
I've been to the vaccination clinics with a newcomer Afghan family, and more recently to the front desk to ask a question. Each and every interaction I have had with a nurse (at least 5) or a receptionist (at least 3) at Public Health has been friendly, respectful, professional and effective. I'm very impressed with the staff, and their knowledge, skill and customer service. Thank you!

The nurse who gave multiple vaccines to our twin boys, has done an excellent job. She was quick, caring and possess good knowledge. My wife and I just love the hospitality and care that our boys received here. Recommend 100%

I had a 1 o’clock appointment I checked in talked to the front receptionist who was a skinny black woman. I told her I had a 1 o’clock appointment and she told me she was going to call The nurse to let her know that I’ve arrived. I was sitting there watching the time by 1:15. No one came to talk to me so I got up and told the receptionist I don’t have all day. What did she tell me? Oh, I forgot to call them. What??? I have autism I like to do things by routine so if I have an appointment at 1 o’clock, I like to keep it on time. I had other errands example I had to run to the pharmacy to pick up my medication for my asthma because I was having an asthma flare. They shouldn’t be hiring lazy people to work as receptionist And who forget to call.  I was not impressed at all by that woman and her short attention span.

To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to express my extreme frustration and disappointment with the way Public Health has handled a serious incident involving my wife.

Two weeks ago, my wife was attacked by a dog. The animal was on a leash, but the owner made no effort to control it. The dog latched onto both of her legs, causing puncture wounds and massive bruising—some the size of baseballs. She went to the hospital and was told that Public Health would be contacting her regarding the incident.

Public Health did reach out the same evening, and there were a few follow-up calls over the two-week period. However, at no point during any of those conversations did anyone mention that she should contact Animal Control—or the police. We were led to believe that Public Health was handling the situation and would contact the dog’s owner. That never happened.

Two weeks later, Public Health called again—not to provide updates, but to ask us if we had seen the dog around. Only then were we informed that no one had contacted the dog’s owner, despite having the address. We were also told this matter actually falls under Animal Control’s jurisdiction.

Why wasn’t this communicated earlier? Why go through the motions of follow-up calls if your department has no authority to act?

This has been a complete failure of communication and responsibility. If we had been told on Day One to call Animal Control or the police, we would have. Instead, we’ve lost valuable time, and the owner continues to walk the same dog near children on their way to school.

I don’t usually complain, but this has been handled so poorly that I’m compelled to speak up. We trusted that Public Health would either take action or direct us to the proper authorities. That did not happen.

I strongly recommend that your staff receive proper training to ensure that citizens in urgent situations are directed to the right resources immediately. The safety of the public depends on it—and frankly, so does your credibility.

Do better.

Sincerely,
Alistair McCreedy

Always a pleasurable experience  with all the employees in this unit for over 2 yrs now as required.
Friendly, knowledgeable and helpful service. A true listening skilled bunch here!
Shout out to Inspector Tim Barney who has been an excellent advisory/coach above all else, and advisory officer Joanne.
Thankful for all your services.

name: TNO - The Neighbourhood Organization (Flemingdon)
description:
I decided to give this place a try. I never knew that the staff is with Access To Housing by Elm Street. I applied housing with Access To Housing since from January 2023. I didn't hear nothing yet. Then I went to see a person at the place by last year in July then the person says I have to wait for housing in 7 years. That is way too long. So long story short I spoked to the housing worker from this place and she wanted to advise me that I should go to Peter street to wait for housing. I think that is mean and unfair.

Nice staff...helpful and effective

I’m very disappointed with Ms. Siham Abosita’s behaviour.  I sent her my updated documents just checking , she promised me to help me but she is disappeared since 25 Nov till now. She is not answering my calls, not responding back on text messages, WhatsAap messages, voicemail messages etc. I lost almost one week because of her. I asked her if she is unable to assist just let me know so I could  find alternate but she never respond me.  I don’t know if there is check and balance in the branch she is working.

Im thank you so much to mariya nurhussain, I don't know what will be my life and my 2 kids without this sweet lady, she help me to get subsidy housing, and she patiently listening to my troubles, I almost lose hope before I found this place TNO Gateway boulevard, and thank you
, to the police officer Charles and the officer lady who reference me to yellow brick shelter and yellow bric shelter reference me to TNO Gateway boulevard, thanks to all of you who safe my life and my kids life

Excellent place to come in and find out information regarding settlement in Ontario. Providing legal, immigration, housing, health, employment, rent bank, education, and many more services for newcomers, seniors, children, and marginalized sector. Services also include anti violence against women and LINC classes to learn English. Once you come in you are welcomed with warm smiles from staff and immediately directed to the correct t service. people who get served by TNO always return to this place as it provides solutions for challenges and barriers facing many citizens in Ontario.

So I was homless and the staff here are so amazing helped us with food and stuff for our dogs the compassion if these ppl are so wonderful it warms my heart how welcoming they where to myself and my husband. Thank you alliance staff for all that u have done for my family

Still waiting to see a psychologist since about August 2018 and never received a closing letter, another party did. With extra information disclosed. I was told I would know beforehand what would be released and what wouldn't. Definitely NOT comfortable there. Especially after voicing concerns and being reassured everything was confidential unless court ordered.

People are very vulnerable, expecting help and it is not treated in a timely matter at all

Very friendly and professional care about well being of our community 💯

Not a good clinic they don’t answer calls unless you waste your day waiting for someone to pickup and the doctors never have time to see you unless your okay waiting months to get an appointment

Though the staffs are cooperative & nice; The biggest problem here is, never ever receive calls from outside. I try always call to book an appointment for medical issues or to contact my family Dr. But, impossible! So, I forced to come in person for booking. It's annoying a double trip.

Great community service.
They offer a free 30 minute Rickshaw ride for seniors. I went on one through Taylor Creek Park.

Everybody was respectful except for the nurse guy called Bilal, he was very disrespectful and not ready for the appointment I had with him and I had to come for the 2nd time and wasn't ready again.

Had a lovely experience today.  Needed a quick tune-up on my bike, but didn't have the confidence to do it myself.  They have a clinic on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 1-5pm.  Super friendly and efficient.  Thank you.

They are impossible to get ahold of or have get back to you . The other school read them fine .I told you they were all on schedual ... I don't appreciate basically  being called a liar. awesome place

description:
Very Rude front desk ladie, she called to tell me that I had an appointment with them ,when I arrived they couldn't find me in the system, the secretary started yelling saying that I didn't have an appointment, then yes,but supposed to be  in a month, I called (45 minutes  waiting ) to cancel the appointment and  the date wasn't same than my , them  they ask me which appointment I want to cancel?🙄because they had scheduled two more appointments on different days and  I didn't know. no one asked me they just scheduled the appointment. 🤨🤨

Been coming to black creek community health centre for 4 years now. (Sheridan and yorkgate) the customer service is phenomenal, the nurse practitioners are so kind, understanding, along with that they take the time to help figure out what is wrong with you. I recommend everyone to come here as they help and educate you.

Extremely poorly managed!!! Non-experienced, rude, and arrogant team!!! Waited for 6.5 hrs to get my covid vaccine. There were people cutting in lines because the team didn’t manage well and they only spoke to you very rudely.

Was trying to get my covid vaccine here. They started at 4pm but I came at 1pm and got an appointment/sticker. The clinic is located in a mall and they don't allow line ups, so the security asked us to come back after 4pm. We were reluctant to leave because there were still ppl lining up, but the security guys insisted (but not everyone left). Then we came back at 3pm and the team asked us to line up downstairs outside the mall. Only seniors can stay inside. It was freezing cold but we did what they told us to. There was a super long line outside. We waited for 1.5 hrs outside, and was told that there were young people lining upsairs. We saw people who came late lining in front of us because they refued to go downstairs. When you ask the clinic (in a very polite way) why they don't manage, all they said is "I am not talking to you. Go downstairs".

By the way, they also don't give you receipt because they think it's taking time. They don't allow you to ask questions.

This is the worst clinic I've ever been to. Oh by the way, if you are not from this community, they think it has already been a previlege for you to get vaccine from them. So you just shut up and wait for other people cutting in lines.

Forgot to mention that there is completely no social distancing.

If you want to stay healthy and happy, stay away from this clinic.

It was excellent service, they treated me very well

Centre de santé communautaire de l'Estrie – Alexandria
description:
My mother is 93 years old and her doctor went on indefinite sick leave.
My Mom is healing from a broken wrist and cannot leave her home right now.
I have been trying to get her prescription renewed for almost a month. She is running out this week.  I was reassured twice last week that it would be taken care of last Friday.
It was not done and when I called the clinic again today they made all kinds of excuses for the doctor.
Trying to help my 93 year old Mom.

Not a walk in clinic

advice,

don't lock your doors at 350pm, when yours hours of operation are till 430pm!

Staff is always welcoming!The place is very clean!

Best Clinic

name: Centre de santé communautaire de l'Estrie – Crysler
description:
My NP Dominique Swabey is back and she is absolutely AMAZING. She has done more for me than anyone I have ever dealt with. A good bedside manner and really cares for her patients. We need more of that in this world today. I think she is AMAZING!!!

I've only had positive experiences in this clinic and I am grateful that we have one so close to home.  The front desk staff have always been kind to me, and I have had positive experiences with the nurse-practitioners and the doctor - and I never feel rushed.  Waiting times compared to other doctors I've had have been reasonable, and they do what they can to accommodate your special circumstances.

You can never get a same week appointment, let alone a same month appointment. You might get lucky to get a virtual appointment via phone call in 3 weeks from the time you call. Receptionist is very rude and rarely answers the phone or returns your call in a decent time frame. Very unfortunate as it is so close to my house.

As a member of the LGBT community, I did not feel my concerns were addressed fairly or properly as I felt a constant bias by the former Nurse Practitioner there. Had to go to another health centre for one my issues because I did not feel comfortable to come here after my experiences.

I have only good things to say about this clinic and its people. When my previous GP retired, I was disappointed to have to travel a half hour to find a new doc, but the travel is a small thing when you find a doctor to like and trust.

Impossible to have prescriptions renewed after following proper steps of having pharmacy fax over details (x4) over a 2 week period in two separate occasions. Numerous calls not returned byDr. Lux. . Much decline in service compared to Sudbury location.  Three sites should amalgamate to one and work as a  team for all patients. Such a waste of money and resources and patients are suffering over this set up. Note: Receptionist very efficient,  thank  you Ashley, however management needs to get involved and rectify these inefficiencies with this particular doctor.

Not listened to by nurse prac. Prescribed things that didn't relate to the issue at hand. Hard to get in contact, says open but answering machine says "call when open". Just overall disappointing and frustrating to get what you want/need done.

Terrible service…impossible to make appointments, I’ve made several appointments via phone call & the doctor never calls. It’s absolutely ridiculous. If you care about your health, change doctors

Not to impress about services,  don't answer the phone and don't reply to fax send by pharmacy. Just don't want to work 🤬🤬🤬

name: Centre de santé communautaire du Témiskaming – Earlton
description:
I love going to the health unit when I need to see my doctor. You can easily book appointments online or call. I always get an appointment quickly and the staff are incredible. Thank you.

Pros all the way!

name: Centre de santé communautaire du Témiskaming – Kirkland Lake
description:
I would have given it 5 stars but their receptionist is very rude & loves to cut people off when they are trying to talk. Other than her, the place is great!

Never answers the phone, and booked me with a no book.
Closes on a Friday at one, can call all morning and never receive an answer.

Where I seek medicsl assistance

name: Centre de santé communautaire du Témiskaming – Larder Lake
description:
The ladies are kind and caring.

Very helpful and friendly!!

Lovely staff

Amazing

name: Centre de santé communautaire du Témiskaming – Virginiatown
ConnectWell Community Health – Beachburg Site
description:
Great place, they go out of their way to take good care of you, very friendly and helpful staff.

I have been waiting for a prescription for a good week. Had the pharmacy fax the clinic twice. I have called the clinic a few times and still no script. This is a prescription for high blood pressure. It should not take that long. Very dissatisfied

Very happy with it

name: Country Roads Community Health Centre – Westport
description: Homey chain eatery serving breakfast all day & other American comfort fare in a casual space.

name: East Mississauga Community Health Centre (Satellite of Lamp CHC)
description:
I've been going here for a long time now and all the staff has always been excellent!! The front desk ladies (especially Marlena), the nurses, doctors (Roseanne, Svetlana, Kim) they are AMAZING ❤️ They are true, dedicated professionals. I can say they truly care about their clients. They make you feel important by focusing on you and genuinely helping with whatever clinical needs you have. I do recommend this place 100%, my whole family has been here for different reasons over the years and we have always been satisfied with the care and attention we receive. Thanks so much for being excellent and exceptional at what you do!!

This is a great place for receiving healthcare for transgender people. However they deadname me every time they call me on the phone even though I changed my legal name a year ago and told them.

Today, I had a very disappointing experience at the Clinic. Despite arriving 30 minutes before my scheduled appointment time, I was told that I was late when I went to see the hygienist for my son's appointment, which was near closing time. I explained that I had arrived early, but the staff claimed that their computer showed my arrival time as one hour later. I'm confused and frustrated as to why the front desk lady didn't update the system accurately, especially since she had acknowledged my presence earlier.

I’ve been going here weekly for about 3 years and have yet to be disappointed. They take the safety of their patients seriously and have a wide variety of healthcare services available depending on your needs. The team is compassionate and helpful and their community events offer a lot. Excellent people who will help you to the best of their abilities here.

This place is a joke, from the moment you walk in and still having to wear a mask despite it being mid-2023 where you don't even have to wear masks at hospitals anymore, to the front desk people who are completely clueless to what's going on and just want their shift to ene so they can go home, to the doctors themselves who do bad work because they aren't getting paid enough due and have no relationship with customers and don't care about the center because it's not like a normal dentist business, my mother who's a senior got a filling done, a month later the filling came out, when we tried to book an emergency appointment they told us they can't see her until a month later... Despite this place being free even though it's funded by people's taxes doesn't mean you have the right to do bad work and treat people badly. We will be saving up our money and going to a real health center dentist and be paid customers at least we get treated better and the dental work is competent.

name: Langs – North Dumfries Community Health Centre Satellite
description:
The staff including the receptionist's are awesome and helpful. Dr. Mcgill is kind and genuine. I get my appointments in a timely manner. To the ones complaining about getting same day appointments, it's rare to get a same day... If it can't wait...go to the emergency at the hospital.

Absolutely unacceptable experience. My wife called with an urgent medical concern and was treated with complete rudeness and disregard by the receptionist. At first, she was told a doctor would call her back. When she followed up, she was told no doctors were available because they were “all on vacation.” When she asked if another doctor could help, she was flatly told it was not possible.
This kind of behaviour is appalling in a medical office, where patients come in vulnerable and often anxious about their health. To dismiss someone in need with such attitude and provide zero options for urgent care is unprofessional, negligent, and dangerous. Patients deserve respect, compassion, and proper care—not to be brushed off and spoken to rudely.
I would not recommend this office to anyone who values their health and expects even a basic level of professionalism.

The medical receptionist, Dr McGill, and the medical team are wonderful. I have always been treated with respect and care. I highly recommend this office.

Dr. Mcgill is incredibly kind, empathetic and remarkable, listens patiently, provides complete advice and discusses all options.  Treats patients with care and affection.

I had been a patient since this Health Centre opened 20 years ago. Over the years things have changed dramatically.
I began looking for a new family doctor when the first appointment with my doctor would be in 7 weeks and when I called in for a same day appointment they were always booked up (although I called each time at 8:30 am when their lines opened and was put on hold).
The receptionist response if you needed to see a doctor was....go to a walk in clinic or the hospital emergency room. When I questioned the length of time until a could book an appointment her response was "that's our health care system".

So many issues that I won't write on a public review.

Luckily I found a new family doctor who actually wants to see her patients and I can book an appointment with-in a week of calling.

name: London Intercommunity Health Centre – Argyle Site
description:
The Iraqi-Canadian lady at the front desk was very friendly and helpful.Ali is super professional and helped me so much .Such great team in this clinic. Thank you All

The website is pretty much broken, I tried to send an email by clicking the link, and the page just turned blank

Kind people there

name: North Lambton Community Health Centre – Kettle Point
North Lambton Community Health Centre – West Lambton Community Health Centre
description:
I have been a client from the beginning. They have everything you need there. Social workers, counceling, programs, along with nurses and doctors.
My nurse is Cheryl Degroot. She is one of the most dedicated person I know. Not only does she work in office but in the mobile out reach that helps the whole community. I trust in her more than anyone. She listens. She should be awarded for her hard work and dedication in this community.

I’ve been a patient here for over 15 years. Any Dr, NP,  nurse and office staff I’ve encountered have treated me with the utmost respect and care.  They work with you and figure out a way for you to get an appt if you need seen quickly. They will listen to you and book longer appointments if you have multiple issues. I never feel rushed. I would give them 100 stars if I could :)

Office staff was fine however the nurse practitioner was a different issue. The practitioner assumes ppl to be addicts by the past prescriptions without listening or caring about issues and Refuses to answer questions or look at the clients because she has her own opinions of ppl before beginning interviews.  While complaints to the office failed and are turned to being the clients fault because theyre nervousness from the insulting 1st interview with the practitioner because of lack of decent medical staff in our area?
All I can say to the staff there is a leopard can't change they're spots!  Please be concerned
If a staff member can act one way with staff and another with clients ppl should be concerned

My experience was very poor at West Lambton, my doctor was not knowledgeable in the areas I was needing help and did things that caused more harm than good. The doctor also saw my 6 month old, she was extremely dismissive of symptoms my baby was having. My baby is allergic to dairy (full body hives, diarrhea, vomiting) this doctor brushed off concerns about these symptoms twice, not until we saw a different doctor was she diagnosed with dairy allergy. I expressed concern to said doctor as well to the director with no resolution. They would not allow me to switch doctors within west lambton even though you can call and see any doctor available, so I derostered. The doctor admitted she was more comfortable with elderly patients, don't take on a baby patient if it's not your area of expertise.

The staff are kind, caring and knowledgeable, and im not just saying that! The health care professionals at West Lambton Community Health Centre changed my life for the better. I could cry 🥹 THANK YOU FOR EVERYTHING!!!!!

name: Norwest Community Health Centres – Longlac
Parkdale Community Health Centre – 27 Roncesvalles Avenue
Pinecrest-Queensway Community Health Centre – South Nepean Community Health Centre
description:
I have nothing but great things to say about the nurses who work here. They are thorough and very caring. For example, a nurse entertained my three month old baby so I could have an appointment that was without distraction with my nurse practitioner Elizabeth wiseman.

Our experience with this place was extremely disappointing. Despite booking an appointment for Infant Hearing through their website, we arrived to find that the center was closed during their stated business hours. The sign on the door indicated they were closed on Thursday mornings, which contradicted the appointment availability on their website. When we attempted to seek assistance by knocking on the door, we were met with indifference from the staff inside, who seemed more interested in their own conversation than addressing our concerns. We knocked door again, but the staff inside continued their conversation and ignored us with the newborn baby wandering in front of the door. The lack of professionalism and disregard for their scheduled appointments left us feeling frustrated and undervalued as clients.

Had enough of this place. Unfortunately, it's not easy to find a new family doctor in the city so I have to stick it out. I dread calling in for any reason, because what could be resolved in 2mins will be dragged on for up to 4 weeks going around in circles. As others have said, the admin is terrible and so are the nurses who I've encountered over the phone. Referrals get lost and I constantly have to follow up. There is no proper triaging of your concern when you call (if you even get through) which makes for unnecessarily long wait times. No issues with my primary care provider whatsoever- I wish I didn't have to go through so much before speaking to her. My family and I have been with this CHC since we arrived in Canada 12 yrs ago and nothing has changed. I wish we could call ourselves lucky for having a primary care provider, but this place makes us question that.

Have been with this clinic for the longest time.  The doctors are absolutely excellent.  They are caring, understanding and thorough. The receptionists and nurses are helpful, professional and efficient.  Nothing is perfect in this world and so it goes that doctor availability as well as nurse practioner availability is the issue with South Nepean Health Centre.  Just not enough hands to meet the demand.  You can't be seen when you really want or need to be seen.  It is the waiting that tends to drive me to walk in clinics.   It would be great, if this health centre had an additional walk in service and more evening hours besides Tue and Wed.

I called to make an appointment, the person answers me and hangs up, even she didn't mention  can't hear you, what kind of customer service are you offering? That's not professional, and the second person told me many things when I complained. Horrible customer service

name: Pinecrest-Queensway Health and Community Services
description:
I have been a client for 33 years. I have to say the current staff is the best so far. Ariel at the front desk is amazing, compassionate and goes the extra mile to make sure you get what you need done. I would give her 5 stars too if I could.

Thought I’d pop by and leave a review for the new parents that are bringing their baby in for their newborn hearing assessment as the rest of the reviews aren’t that positive and they may cause some anxiety. My husband was working so I brought the baby in solo! We had our appointment today at 11:30 with Jana. The receptionist was very friendly and directed us to the waiting area upon our arrival. Jana was amazing ! She was very thorough and gave me all the information I needed. She took extra time with my son as he was breathing loudly and was a little fussy. Rest assured that she will do everything she can to complete this test so you don’t have to rebook. I know there is so much pressure to keep your baby asleep, and general anxiety about the test/ results. You can only do so much as a parent. I hope this review helps at least someone put their mind at ease :)

Ive been getting alot of help from Madison Rodney, she has been amazing with finding me solutions for fitting in the right job feild using nessasary resources to help guide me to where I need to be in the proper workplace. Five Stars for Madison Rodney because she is super awsome and so caring. And Jasmine at the front counter is so cool to help schedule my appointments on days that best work for me, you couldn't ask for a better pair of Fantastic helpers.

The receptionist was extremely rude and unhelpful, it felt like the last thing was wanted to do was help people entering the community center! Don’t bother speaking to her for help if you come here, you’re better off finding your way on your own.

About a month ago I was waiting in their reception when I young black mom came in and asked a question in French.
The receptionist replied in English that she didn’t speak French. The mom struggled to speak in English but I ended up helping her get the info she needed.

Then last week I was sitting in the reception again waiting for playgroup and that same receptionist replied in perfect French to a phone call.

Other then leaving this review here I’m not sure what else I can do. :(

I was so disappointed in her. Especially in this building.

name: Rexdale Community Health Centre – Jamestown Hub
description:
Great services,today I went their even they have local produce to sell

Loved this place, the clinic is wonderful, the doctor is very nice and helpful, the nurse, Simone is so attentive and speaks Spanish very fluently. They always called two days before your appointment to remind you about it. They offer different programs prenatal, postnatal, kids and senior activities.  It’s a very clean facility, and got lots of parking on the back!

We took a simple room and turned it into a warm and welcoming environment. This is an awesome community centre. The customer service prior to and on the day of our event was great & we were able to host the event without a glitch. Thumbs up! Thanks for an amazing experience!

I went there to go get my Ontario Id but first I had to get my birth certificate before I get Ontario Id as proof of Identification so I was asked questions filled out forms now have to wait 6-8 weeks or even earlier before I have to go pick up my birth certificate there then they will book me to get my Ontario Id someone who works there will take me to service Ontario to cover the price for the I'd and take my picture and wait 4-6 weeks to pick it up or send in the mail the lady that served me was very nice if you go here to get your id you have to go on Mondays between 1-3 overall it's a good place for any help for legal matters and kids and adult and seniors programs it only took me a little over 30 minutes to get there from my house by Jane and Steele's

Rexdale Community Hub is a jewel in the heart of Etobicoke. Great atmosphere, serene and quiet. Excellent Sunday morning family get together. Booking with Vicki is painless and quite professional. Muhammed, the sign in person is professional and personable. I cannot remember any negative experience with staff at this location. I would highly recommend for event gathering or meetings.
JG

name: Univi Community Health Centre - Noelville
address: 44 St-Christophe Street Unit 3, Noelville, ON P0M 2N0

address: 1 King Street, St. Charles, ON P0M 2W0

name: Univi Community Health Centre – Warren
address: 39 Lafontaine Street Unit 5, P.O. Box 38, Warren, ON P0H 2N0
description:
Once you see a n.p. Service is excellent, however the problem is getting into see someone in an emergency.  I have been told once it would take a month to get an appointment. That was  for a shoulder injury.  Last week I  was told to go to a walk in clinic.  Maybe  they should allow  a certain amount of time for emergency.

Everyone involved in patient care at Univi Health is concerned with the health and we'll being of each patient. Tests are scheduled quickly and results are communicated quickly. They are compassionate and knowledgeable and kind. I would recommend them to anyone!

Very friendly office nurse practitioner are helpful up to there training but sometimes wait to long for assistance from doctor

name: West Toronto Community Health Services – Dundas Street
address: 3446 Dundas Street West, Toronto, ON M6S 2S1

name: Windsor Essex Community Health Centre – Street Health
description:
My husband and I, also my youngest daughter and her 4 children have been going there for 9 years on Pelissier and now University.  They have been very helpful, friendly and courteous.  Couldn't do without them.
Thanks guys 😀

Wonderful staff, very helpful and very accommodating! In a difficult time I spoke with the lovely staff at the front desk, who were able to go above and beyond to help me. Maisa and her colleague were able to provide me a solution, quickly, confidently and with kindness. I greatly appreciate both of these women and would highly recommend this center. Thank you again!

I've been seeing Denise who is a nurse practitioner for 7+ years, she's very attentive, and personable. There's never a long wait which tells me they respect my time; I've been able to make my appointments to suit my work schedule.

Called the office on a whim to inquire about medical services available to non-resident, uninsured patients. Carole was emphatic to my unique circumstance and took the time to thoroughly explain all my options. After we hung up, Carol clarified one of my questions with the medical staff and immediately called back with their response. Carol's thoughtfulness, responsiveness and willingness to help a complete stranger made my day!

Ive been a patient for 2 or 3 years now and they are professional and caring.
They have helped me through a few incidents and deffinately i love my doctor/nurse for her kindness and helping me through the embarrassing situations.
She is deffinately the best ive ever had and enjoy knowing im in good hands.

name: Woolwich Community Health Centre – Wellesley Township Community Health Centre
address: 1180 Queen's Bush Road, P.O. Box 187, Wellesley, ON N0B 2T0
description:
Really hard to get a appointment, so plan months in advance if you want any care. Receptionist can be ether really nice or not care at all. Doctor and nurse can be ether be extreme on booking tests or have zero follow up. So you ether have every kind of test imaginable, witch can be great if you have something needing to be figured out. But it can also be a bit extreme and come with a lot of anxiety. Or you come with a problem get one test done and never get a follow up.

Extremely kind and helpful health care workers! Options for people who don’t have a family doctor yet.

Doctor Will is awesome, but the front desk is IMPOSSIBLE to get a hold of, don't bother calling you'll never get through. It's extremely annoying

They apitamy of medical sexism and ignorance. They only do anything when it is life threatening or if it could reflect how poor their service is.

Best doctor,best nurse I’ve been with this clinic for 9 years doctor William is very good doctor he was are family doctor,If I live in Wellesley more I never looking for any doctor.I going to miss you

name: CANES Community Care - Intensive Senior Community Team (ISCT)
address: 10 Carlson Court, Suite 200, Etobicoke, ON M9W 6L2
description:
Loved our experience with Elvis! He is so pleasant and lovely and did a fantastic job installing the grab bars and hand rail in our home for my mother. Highly recommend !

Today I had safety bars for the tub installed for my parents. Elvis the installer was amazing! He was fast, efficient and pleasant. I would recommend his services to all who need the bars installed. Thanks Elvis I appreciate your professionalism and hard work. A bientot Steve

Although they had a couple of issues regarding timing, the installation and customer service was great. Elvis attended to install a couple grab bars and walked me through the entire install process as well. Highly recommend.

Had safety bars installed in my bathtub and at front door. Job was completed by Elvis in a most professional and friendly manner. Cannot say enough about the wonderful experience he provided  - thoroughly knowledgeable and competent in completing this task.
Would highly recommend him!

I utilized their expertise and services for installing  grab bars for my front entrance on 02/08/2024. David arrived in time and did the work in a most efficient and professional manner. Very knowledgeble and knew what exactly he is doing. Would recommend him to anyone needing their services in GTA.

name: SEAS Centre - Toronto - North York Centre
description: Conveniently located, accessible by bus number 25 (Don Mills). It's situated south of Steeles, at the corner of Cliffwood Plaza. This community center can accommodate approximately 20 people; it's not large, but air-conditioned in the summer. If air conditioning isn't needed, the doors can be opened during class to allow for air circulation.

name: Strides Toronto - Galloway After School Program (GASP)
Taddle Creek Family Health Team - Bay and College Site - Office of Drs Lemos, Vainberg, Chung, Segal and NP Joanna Peddle
description:
I went several times to this clinic and they were great professionnals ! They have a wonderful receptionist and the nurses are really doing their jobs well. They are attentive and empathetic to their patients.

Best experience I’ve ever had in a doctors office and with a doctor. The quality of care here is truly wonderful and my life has benefitted as a result of that care immensely. Will always be thankful for this place.

They don’t answer their phone so it’s very hard to get appointments booked. My Doctor moved here and it’s become very difficult to actually get any healthcare now

Dr. Valentinis has been my GP for many years. Over the last few months, I have experienced serious health concerns and she has made the logistics of forms, insurance and employers as easy and smooth as possible. The admin staff is wonderful and super accommodating.

When you are able to get through, the quality of care is great (truly interprofessional team, top notch); however the booking process to get an appointment is a bit like hunger games in the morning. You have to call at 8.30; wait on the line (often up to 20-30 minutes) to be told all the appointments are booked and you need to repeat the same thing the next day until you get through for same-day appointments.

name: Friends and Advocates Peel
address: 239 Queen St E, Unit 6, Brampton, ON L6W 2B6

name: Hospital for Sick Children (The) - Adolescent Substance Use Program
description:
The world renowned Sick Kids hospital has been doing incredible work for children's health. Very large and well managed hospital. I took a child to emergency and didn't wait forever to be seen by the nurse but had to wait for the doc. Come on, health care is free in our beautiful country, so pack your patience. If I ever do stumble across a pot of gold, or a lotto win, I would donate to them as I noticed the washroom near emergency could use a modern renovation. Great bedside and patient centred approach.
Lots of good places for variety of food including Timmies, Starbucks, Sushi, Subway etc.

Went to see my niece recently. Hospital is huge. It is nicely decorated to spread some holiday cheer. Staff are super helpful.
I have visited previously with my own teenager and we got lost on our way out. We asked a person who actually walked us out towards the exit. It turned out we had asked someone important for directions. Unfortunately, we didn't get the name and she brushed off our thank yous graciously.
Shoutout to gift shop folks too.

We came here this morning for an emergency. When you enter from the underground parking and take the elevator, there is no sign or guidance showing which floor the emergency department is on. We only found out later that it’s on the ground floor.

Overall, the emergency services were good and fast. Thanks to the staff here!

We visited the SickKids Hospital Cardiac Clinic and had a very positive experience overall. We had to travel nearly two hours to get there and arrived about 45 minutes earlier than our scheduled appointment. Despite being early, the staff were incredibly accommodating and quickly arranged for the echo and ECG tests to be done right away, which saved us a lot of waiting later.

There was a bit of a delay in seeing the doctor. In fact, it was even a bit later than our original appointment time but we understand that specialists like cardiac doctors can be very busy, and we truly appreciate the time they dedicate to each patient. Once we did meet the doctor, the consultation was thorough, informative, and reassuring.

The staff were kind, professional, and efficient throughout the visit. The clinic environment is clean, organized, and very child-friendly, which helped ease any stress or anxiety. In and out, it was a smooth process and worth the long drive. We’re grateful for the care and attention we received and would definitely recommend this clinic to other families seeking pediatric cardiac care.

A truly positive experience at one of the best hospitals for children in Ontario.

Brought our 20 month old in this morning at 3am to the ER. Nobody was in the waiting room, didn’t get seen until 8am and it was by a Nurse Practitioner. We were sent home with “we don’t know why his fever is at 40, come back in a couple days”. When I was walking to the bathroom, I noticed the nurses station had Netflix playing on their computer while a good handful of RNs were watching The Resident episodes. Great job filtering through your staff there, Sick Kids. Do better.

name: Pharma Docs Downtown
description:
been renting here for more than a year. great building. well taken care of. friendly staff overall. some of the units are cheaply made though. lots of residents had issues with windows (i had a drafty window where you could feel cold air come in, though property manager was fast to fix it). studios in front of elevators get a lot of noise, as the doors are paper thin. the outdoor patio is freaking awesome. location is unbeatable but neighbourhood is still seedy. id say its still somewhat safe for young women but there are certainly better neighbourhoods out there. west facing views on 42nd floor are gorgeous and unobstructed.

Charming Lobby, Cozy Living

82 Dalhousie St offers:

Pros:

•⁠  ⁠Stunning lobby, beautiful design
•⁠  ⁠Great location, walkable Yonge-Dundas square
•⁠  ⁠Friendly staff, secure building

Cons:

•⁠  ⁠Apartments are smaller compared to similar buildings
•⁠  ⁠Limited natural light in some units
•⁠  ⁠New building but some outdated fixtures and finishes

Suggestions:

•⁠  ⁠Update units with modern amenities
•⁠  ⁠Add more storage options
•⁠  ⁠Enhance outdoor spaces

Overall, 82 Dalhousie St is a charming building with compact living spaces.

Rating Breakdown:

•⁠  ⁠Lobby/Amenities: 5/5
•⁠  ⁠Location: 5/5
•⁠  ⁠Apartment Size: 2.5/5
•⁠  ⁠Overall Experience: 3/5

Terrible management—unresponsive, unhelpful, and disorganised. Calls, even for emergencies, go unanswered, and their so-called office is either closed or they refuse to open the door. The study room and lounge are inaccessible unless you pay a $200 deposit each time.
They gave a misleading 12-hour notice to change the locks, did it while I was away, and left me locked out. When I sought help, the concierge, Mishel, refused to issue new keys unless I provided a physical Xerox of my ID. A digital copy or physical ID wasn’t enough and asked me to look for a copier to get xerox, and she even stated they don’t take responsibility for document security. Concierge staff change constantly, give conflicting information, and are outright rude and disrespectful. Emails and in-person instructions contradict each other, causing endless frustration. Their newsletter claims they welcome recommendations, but no one is available to listen, and nothing is ever considered. The apartments are extremely small, and security is a joke. The overall experience of living here is frustrating. Instead of making residents lives easier, management seems determined to make them worse. Save yourself the headache—look elsewhere.

I would give negative stars if I could. This is going to be a long one so keep some tine aside to read this. 82 Dalhousie/199 Church is worst thing you can do to yourself. The management is the most unprofessional and illogical people I have dealt with in my life. Every interaction has been me pulling my hair.
I have lived here for a year and it has been a nightmare - it caused me health issues i.e migraines. 1. When I first moved in it was a new building so the false fire alarms would go off at random times 4 AM, 5 AM, anytime and the concierge would repeat the announcement a 1000 times because they kept stuttering. So say goodbye to your sleep.
2. They changed the keys and elevator access out of nowhere and the fun fact- the Fob gives you access to any floor - the irony.
3. My apartment flooded unfortunately due to the washer malfunction on a weekend- what did the management do? “It’s the weekend, we can’t help, sorry” they didn’t even bother to check in on Monday if the apartment was still flooded.
4. $200 deposit and $25 fee to use the study rooms. Yep, gotta take a trip to the bank to book a study room with a “certified cheques”. Pay the rent + pay for the amenities (just give your entire property to them)
5. Uneducated management - we made a noise complaint on our floor for another unit and management ended up sending our landlord a WARNING for us - causing us to almost getting evicted.

Amenities that you pay for with your condo fee all require additional cost to use. They run fire alarm tests at ridiculous times (pre 8am) with no respect for the residents trying to live and enjoy their space. Units are also small compared to others in the area for similar prices.

description:
these people are thieves!!! I paid them 99 dollars to help me 3 years ago and I received no help whatsoever! then for 2 years they have brazenly gone into my visa and taken 99 dollars without my permission!!!

I paid 99$ membership fee and was told it was for a year but they automatically charged me the next year. Nobody answers the phone during opening hours. Their website link is broken.

Great service for landlords in need. Highly recommend.

This service is a joke. Why would the so called "Self-help Center" be closed on Wednesdays while tribunals are being held. Landlords are on their own to navigate the needlessly confusing L.T.B. paperwork. The whole system is nothing short of pathetic.

Catherine helped me with evicting my tenant. She is an ANGEL. Such a nice lady and helpful. She deserves a raise plus some.

name: Yorktown Child and Family Centre
description: MANAGEMENET GOOD

Save your money and avoid Bartimeaus if you’re a group home looking for staff to fill your shifts. Bartimeaus charges an astronomical amount of money and sends you some of the laziest, and most entitled “staff” you’ll ever see. The staff we were sent refused to take any direction, did no work, and would often cause conflict with our own in-house staff. Not worth the cost.

Waste of time, they don’t show up

Kinark Centre-Est
description: We had a very negative experience and their facility including and specifically the OT room is underwhelming.

name: Dr. Tim Bilkey
Dr. Tim Bilkey

description:
We have been here multiple times to see Doctor Francisco. He is a wonderful doctor-patient.He takes time to talk to you and make sure you understand everything and all his staff are friendly polite i would recommend this place to anyone.

Fix the after hours urgent care you cannot get through to talk to anyone to get into see a dr, so I tried the Telehealth you state in your automated message & I literally have a 4hr wait before a nurse actually calls me back! Is this the only way to do urgent care now? It states your open at both locations until 730pm & have no way of talking to anyone when I’ve called numerous times! Am I missing something? Last year I never had an issue getting through with after hours for my kids but now…. Eeesh 😳

Just wanted to share my experience with the phone service. I called this morning and the young woman who answered was very helpful and called me back to clarify an issue I had inquired about. She had outstanding customer skills. I wanted to commend her to admin because she was so helpful and capable!  Things must have changed since the negative feedback I read from earlier. Very personable young person!!!

Does anyone who work at Erie Shores know that the Urgent Care calendar is wrong and it’s impossible to book an appointment? The days, calendar dates and times are not aligned

For example, Thursday March 4, 2024 8AM - 5 PM in Kingsville.
First of all, March 4th is a Monday
Secondly, since it’s a Monday, it’s probably not an after hours date
Third point is that it is probably meant to be a Saturday date which would be March 2nd or 9th.

I tried booking an appointment back in January and assumed that it was just a rollover error from 2023 to 2024.  After 2 months it’s still wrong so, my new assumption is that nobody said anything or whomever is in charge of it, needs to see a doctor, if they think it’s OK as is.

This health team is great once you get past the miserable admin staff that answer the phones. Don't expect basic phone manners when you call to ask questions. I would give the health team a 5 star rating (only because the doctors and nurses are great) but every-time my experience is completely ruined by the reception staff. This really should be addressed by management. It costs zero dollars to be nice...

name: Dr. Jacqueline Raynard
description:
We have had the pleasure of working with Elizabeth and Dr. Rynard in a diagnosis for my son. They did their work diligently and answered all the questions we had. Even a month after receiving the diagnosis this office has still been helpful in questions and filling out our DTC forms. I would recommend this office to anyone seeking help or a diagnosis in a heart beat. Thank you for everything.

Dr.Jacqueline Rynard

I would be very careful when dealing with this doctor she could misdiagnosis your child causing medications that they don't need. She let an abusive mother with multiple CAS and police reports have the rein over the decision making where my child was concerned meaning my child is going to be put on medication because of a misdiagnosis.

I would not wish this on anybody's children let alone my own goodluck if you have to deal with her.

Listens well, reassures, guides

name: Dilico Anishnabet Family Services - Marathon District Office
Dilico Anishnabet Family Services - Longlac District
Dilico Anishnabet Family Services - Nipigon District Office
description: This is by far the worst CFS service agency (nipigon location) I have ever had communication with. These people do not know me literally as I am not the parent accused of child safety issues and don't even live in ont. These people called me and claimed I was mentally unstable that I shouldn't have children in my care. Again These ppl do not know me and have no way of knowing me other then from the parent who's child came forward about safety issues at home. This organization made a decion on the state of the child's home BEFORE ever interviewing the child. This child had only spoken to the after hour line and reported directly to the on call.worker what her concerns were. Keep your kids safe because these cfs workers will NOT. The accusations from the child were of a serious nature and instead of attending the home to see first hand what the child was speaking of instead the manager (I think nipigon) made the call to instead make a phone call to the.parent who kept this child in harms way in the 1dt place where cfs told the mother what the accusations were. Thus allowing both adults in the home to take steps to hide/get rid of the exact things the child told cfs about. I came to find out that a worker attends thebhome weekly but dmis there for only the younger child and never speaks to the older child. It took me all of 5 min to see something was very wrong with the child who reported the family. I believe that these ppl decided to attack me because I clearly asked them how they cfs could attend the home weekly and not speak to our see the older child. How a cfs worker could miss issues in the home unless they were complete idiots.the child reported things directly to these ppl but during the call the child had a panic attack where I was asked questions at which time I answered but me answering rhe questions they asked turned into me APPARENTLY making the complaint/accusations.  They decided after speaking to me that The child indeed never made any complaints or accusations and it was all.me. this child.told.me that no one would listen, So i took precautions as to protect myself and have the voice of the child heard as to not be blamed for things I did not do. Thank God I did because those precautions I took is what was proven to police that I was telling the truth and that this child made the complaint herself. These same people had the audacity to call.me and verbally attack me and threaten me and then in the same call asked me when I would be flying this child across the country to get her home (child is visting family out of province) Seriously rhe tell.me I'm.mentally unstable, shouldn't have children in my care and now somehow it would be OK fpr me to take this child and fly her across the country. Thank God as soon as I felt something was off about our conversation I started recording th conversation and I will be pursuing a filull investigation into the manager and regional director as to there conduct. Police in my province heard the recording or were stunned at the behavior of these cfs workers. I even had to call a lawyer as I was told that these agency had called local police and that they were sending someone to my home fpr a wellness check and that i was refusing to return this child so i immediately went down to the.local.police told them what happened, insisted that they speak to the child alone without me present in which they did and after they gpt to the bottom of things they se t the child home with me with no concerns of safety.

description: Wow where do you find your workers and how do they qualify??? Am I wrong in insinuating that the point of the organization is to help CHILDREN??? Than why did I have to watch 2 completely heart broken babies earth shatter when I said "no baby you can't come home with me" I have never done anything but right by them. Yet their other who swears, screams and threatens them on a daily can have them. Honestly, they would be better off in care

The Community Center is nice. We hold events like family gatherings weddings anniversaries and a lot more other things. The community center has a a basketball gym has area for making food also holding a little small bar kind of thing. It has a workout area and it's open to be rented by the anyone in the community. If you need any more information you can just go to the office that inside the front doors and/or Band Office

Great First Nation. They got their plans in order and a friendly community.  Miigwetch!

I enjoyed many wonderful classes here and information sessions.

I like the people an the courtesy that I receive,when I walk through the door. The services are there for every need, especially from what I can see.

Great drumming circle experience, great conversation and laughter. Thanx for the welcome!

name: Garden River First Nation Wellness Centre
Indian Friendship Health Centre of Sault Ste. Marie
description:
Thankful for everything that the staff does there !!  Amazing

The services provided here are amazing...not to mention the free lunch that happened to be distributed the day that I stopped here...the hot meal was an awesome surprise and so good!! The staff were very helpful and friendly!!

Great people great service

Great, free music for the community on Thursday nights from 6-9 pm. The band jams out, invites others to come up & sing. Regulars and newcomers that show up. A meal is offered for everyone attending, delicious and nutritious. Families are welcomed, any age can attend.

They offer lots of services and programs! Great place for people who want to get in touch with their culture! I am not native but am always made welcomed and encourage me to get involved. Very helpful in offering assistance whether it is supplies, food, a warm meal every Tuesday and Thursday lunch, Thursday evenings for coffee house., furniture. If you need help, just ask! If they can't help you they will find someone who can!

Miigwetch for such a great experience.

I am not sure exactly her name but I was impressed and grateful that our people have advocates for our children and elders.

Treated like I was important. Very wonderful staff. From the nurse practitioner’s all the way to psychiatrist. They just care. Makes you feel respected Thank You.

Myself and My family Love being a part of this health centre they have everything u could need under one roof and The staff especially Miranda Moore our Health Practitioner is absolutely amazing Friendly helpful staff They all absolutely everyone involved with this great place are amazing helpful friendly caring ect ..

very nice people and a friendly atmosphere

name: NIIJKIWENDIDAA ANISHNAABEKWEWAG SERVICES CIRCLE
description:
I made a report to this agencý out of concern for a child last summer. The person who took my call asked for my name and contact information. I just called the agency a few minutes ago, out of deep concern for a child's safety, who is in their care. It was the most patronizing call. It was meant for me to feel heard, and that was all. The person who took my call, Natalie, wasn't interested in my name and contact information. When I noted to her that I know this isn't how things work, Natalie told me I could leave my name and phone number if I chose to. I told her I'm writing this review, and she's fortunate if I don't edit to paste a link to the recorded conversation, for anyone who reads this to copy and paste to their Facebook search engine for instant access.

Its a wonderful organization that helps women, and their families, in crisis. Great staff, great energy.

Amazing

name: WOMEN'S COMMUNITY HOUSE
description:
Was forced to go there with my 2 young children 2 years ago. When we arrived they gave us a cold meal and let us sit in dining room for hours before they even came to check on us. They finally let us upstairs to our room which had bags on the bed. We left an abusive home so clearly did not have time to pack anything. They acted as if me asking for a pair of pj's to sleep in was an inconvenience to them. The tv in our room was broken and I asked if I could get a new one or trade with a resident that was willing to trade with me, they would say ya we will get to it. Some residents in there were totally amazing while some others weren't. Police were called girl went crazy. One night I couldn't sleep so I decided to go for a smoke while walking downstairs the staff were all sitting in there in the open laughing and discussing past and present people who were and are there. Talking about me me and my situation and laughing about it saying you hear how she sounds entitled like we have to give her a TV. Of course I stood there for a minute to listen and again I heard them talking about my good friend who left there 4 months prior to my arrival and the staff talking extremely bad about her and her children. I of course called my friend and told her. I went for my smoke went back to my room packed our things as I felt so disrespected, I went there for a reason and here the staff are laughing at my situation. After I left the next day my friend called me and said she called the shelter and expressed it wasn't appropriate for them to still be talking about her especially the things they were saying about her. The staff told my friend that I had no business to stand there and be nosey and advised my friend to tell me im not allowed to go back for 6 months. HORRIBLE HORRIBLE PLACE. it took them 4 days to even start my intake.

Sad, this shelter allows abusers inside who are using and drinking. I’m currently being threatened via text by someone currently staying in this shelter and when I called to report the person they said it’s best I call the police. Way to allow abusive woman into a vulnerable place for not only woman but children and let woman use the system for housing when they’re not even abused. Disgusted with what this shelter allows! I hope woman and families inside can be aware of this person they’re not well.

I used to donate to this shelter and one day, I found myself in the same situation as the women who need the service. Unfortunately, the shelter told me to go and find a place and they were very rude.
That was the same woman who was nice a couple months prior.
I got back on my feet by myself with no help and suffered intense situations in London Ontario!

1- I will never donate to you again
2- you lack compassion

Not the feminist organization they claim to be. Whoever runs this place is doing a horrible job, it’s sad really. How they treat their employees and clients goes against all of their mission statements and values.

This is an amazing service to abused women. Not only is Shelter provided, counselors are there to provide guidance, advice, direction and coaching. School bus service for your kids to get to school, childcare counsellors , and an on site chef who cooks your meals. Basically if you are ready to receive help and move forward with your life they have everything you need to succeed. They try to take all the stress off of you so you can get back on your feet. I will always donate to this wonderful organization.

name: ROTHOLME (WOMEN'S AND FAMILY SHELTER)
description:
Do not donate to this place you see workers on a regular basis carrying the food. Pop and what not to their own personal vehicles. It's absolutely run horribly. The staff there are far from on the same page but have all these rules for residents. They should really worry about themselves before they even have residents there.   Highly unprofessional.  Under trained.  On different pages.  Itd actually sad and sickening.

This place is a joke they invade your privacy and over step your parenting and when you tell them please don't they call CAS on you telling them you abuse your children. They take your food and make it their own... and don't get me started on the staff especially the boss pola she is the biggest one of all she kicks out families who have nowhere to go over just disciplining your kids and makes you feel worthless worst place ever for families in need. Don't go to this shelter cause they treat you like your scum....

For a shelter, there actions are absolute disgusting. Especially for people who need help. This place is better shut down than open. Also meeds new management. The manager there clearly does not so any compassion or empathy people in need. The people that work there should be ashamed to stand behind someone like Paola.

My experience with rotholme supervision paola durrand Burrows and housing stability worker has been disgusting i have been a vicitm of professional misconduct and have been discriminated against because I homeschool my child I also had a call placed that was not a child protection concern to london cas and been harassed this past month their staff micheal put a refferal to a program meant to allegedly assist and build goals that has resulted to cause harm trying to get us back in shelture homeless with nothing this is a sick joke these people will be accounted for their actions

I was encouraged by my case worker to return to situations he was aware were abusive.

Considering that it takes several times for people to leave said situations says all it needs to about the lack of skills, knowledge, training, and empathy at Rotholme.

I wish anyone that ends up at this shelter the best allowing their inner guidance to lead them and not the untrained staff.

name: WEENEEBAYKO HEALTH AHTUSKAYWIN
description:
Good   small town service.    ice on a  visiting t movs factory island  very nice local man in that area rust me to hospital in the emergency room. They took an x-ray my leg. And it was a fracture they did immediate cast on it. And he had me a wheelchair to use. A  week later I'm back home in Hamilton. I went to the fashion clinic they looked at x-ray from the hospital moving you look x-rays again and they said my fax filled with all healed up took the cast off return the wheelchair to Hamilton general hospital and they returned it back to hospital and move back to Ontario

The hospital needs more then one Dr. working nights I see and here what patients go through laying in the halls, it's the tone used that reflects on patient's to with some nurses. It is a wonder why people get upset. I did not get the Dr. Name tonight but I have the nurses name. I for one see to many different people being told this then another tells them something else. I was one of these patient's myself it is sad to have no choice to go when your suffering and you feel like your not being heard. I was still hurting especially knowing my person is suffering I do not want to lose another person that I love.
